_comment: Venue registry. open=true means no requirements (any band with a demo can play); open=false is gated by req (minReleases/minFans/minFame/showsInTown/minBuzz). discover=true means the venue must be found in person (Maps 'Head over') before it can be booked; absent/false = known from start. days = weekdays (MO..SU) the venue hosts shows (its 3x/week rhythm); slot = time-of-day (morning/afternoon/evening/late). drawMult scales crowd+pay; payMult scales pay independently (college = big crowd, tiny pay). booker = contact met on discovery. blurb = flavor. Venues without days[] fall back to the legacy open-most-nights rhythm.

ralphs.name: Ralph's Basement
ralphs.blurb: Your buddy Ralph clears the laundry off the couch. BYOB, ~15 people, pays out of the tip jar.

church.name: Church Basement
church.blurb: Folding chairs, fluorescent lights, a sober but attentive crowd. Keep it PG. Small honorarium.

college.name: College House Party
college.blurb: Sticky floors and a keg. Big crowd, broke crowd - great for your name, lousy for cash.

thedive.name: The Dive
thedive.blurb: The real stage in town. Sal books bands with a record out and a following - earn it in the basements first.

saloon.name: The Last Lasso
saloon.blurb: Pokeville's country-only stage. Dusty books acts with a record out and a real following — and no, your noise-punk set won't fly here.
